MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.headline: Q1 FY27 DC $39.0B (+73% YoY); networking $14.9B (+199%); Q2 guided $45B; NVLink Fusion extending ecosystem; CPO roadmap; China zero; 4-way customer concentration persists.

shifts.note: Slight re-acceleration from +68% FY26 full year to +73% Q1 FY27 YoY as Blackwell ramps fully
shifts.trend: DC YoY: FY26 +68% (10-K) → Q1 FY27 +73% (10-Q); Q2 guide $45B implies +66% YoY (normalizing base)

shifts.note: Networking now the fastest-growing segment — NVLink and Spectrum-X both scaling
shifts.trend: Networking: FY26 +162% (10-K) → Q1 FY27 $14.9B (+199% YoY)

shifts.note: 4-way >10% concentration unchanged; NVLink Fusion slightly mitigates by pulling in 3rd-party XPU networking revenue
shifts.trend: Q3 FY26: 4 customers 22/15/13/11% → Q1 FY27: similar pattern expected (4 >10%)

shifts.note: China optionality fully impaired — H20 restricted, H200 tariffed, no compliant product for Chinese data centers
shifts.trend: FY26 H20 effectively zero; Feb 2026 H200 license carries 25% US import tariff; China modeled at zero

shifts.note: Language unchanged across 10-K and Q1 10-Q — energy/capital availability gating deployments
shifts.trend: Both filings cite data-center energy and capital availability as gating constraint — no easing language added

shifts.note: CPO roadmap adds new spend category; Spectrum-X + CPO sampling H2 FY27
shifts.trend: FY26 10-K: $17.5B private/infra investments + $3.5B guarantees; Q1 FY27: CPO Spectrum-X H2 FY27 sampling

NVDA.verdict: Story unchanged — supply-constrained AI capex driving DC growth. NVLink Fusion is the new strategic development: NVIDIA extending its networking moat to cover non-NVIDIA XPU clusters. CPO roadmap signals awareness of power efficiency bottleneck. China optionality remains zero. Maintain bull thesis with 4-way customer concentration as the primary monitoring item.

shifts.trend: Latest filing introduces tightness language absent from prior
shifts.note: auto-derived (new tightness — single-quarter, lower confidence)

shifts.trend: Supply-tightness language present in both latest filings
shifts.note: auto-derived from filing keyword presence

HPE.headline: Server & HPC +38% YoY on AI server demand; AI systems backlog elevated; margin pressure from GPU component mix; GreenLake ARR growing.

shifts.note: AI server ramp driving Server & HPC segment to +38% YoY — fastest growth in years
shifts.trend: Server & HPC: +mid-teens (Q4 FY25) → +38% YoY (Q2 FY26)

shifts.note: AI component costs (GPUs, HBM modules) and hyperscaler pricing pressure compressing gross margin
shifts.trend: Gross margin under pressure from AI component mix; non-AI segments partially offsetting

shifts.note: AI server backlog elevated but converting as supply normalizes; lead times compressing
shifts.trend: AI systems backlog remains elevated; order-to-ship lead times compressing with supply normalization

HPE.verdict: HPE is a pass-through beneficiary of AI infrastructure build — growing revenues but margin pressure from AI component mix limits earnings leverage. GreenLake ARR growth and AI Factory bundling are the strategic differentiators. Monitoring gross margin trajectory as AI mix rises.

MOD.headline: DC +73% YoY to $1.1B in FY26; $4B chiller LTA through 2029 with $165M prepayment; FY27 guided DC +60-80%, EBITDA $650-680M — pure-play cooling thesis fully validated.

shifts.note: Data center mix now dominant; vehicular spin-off will isolate the pure-play DC cooling story
shifts.trend: DC revenue: Q2 FY26 run-rate ~$750M annualized → FY26 actual $1.1B (+73% YoY) → FY27 guided $1.8-2.0B (+60-80%)

shifts.note: Both magnitude and clarity of guidance improved — $4B LTA anchors the revenue bridge through FY29
shifts.trend: FY26 DC exit rate >$400M/Q; FY27 total +20-35%, DC +60-80%, EBITDA $650-680M (+40%+)

shifts.note: Hyperscaler customer locking capacity 3 years forward with prepayment — supply tightness is structural
shifts.trend: $4B LTA + $165M upfront prepayment received in Q4 FY26; backlog extended through 2029

shifts.note: Single hyperscaler LTA implies elevated customer concentration — execution risk if that customer slows
shifts.trend: $4B over 3 years (~$1.3B/year) vs FY26 DC revenue $1.1B — one customer may approach 50%+ of DC

MOD.verdict: Thesis intact and accelerating — the $4B hyperscaler LTA with upfront prepayment is the strongest forward-demand signal in the cooling sub-sector. FY27 EBITDA guide implies >40% growth and margin expansion. Primary risk is hyperscaler customer concentration and execution on Performance Technologies spin-off.

AVGO.headline: AI semi $10.8B (+143% YoY) in Q2; Q3 guided $16B AI semi; FY26 $56B; FY27 >$100B — 6 hyperscaler XPU customers confirmed; beat-and-raise cadence widening; networking ~40% of AI revenue.

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening each quarter; Q2 bookings $30B vs $22.2B revenue implies backlog building
shifts.trend: AI semi: $4.1B (Q1 FY26) → $10.8B (Q2 FY26, +163% QoQ) → $16B guided (Q3 FY26, +48% QoQ)

shifts.note: FY26 AI target moved from $40B+ to $56B; FY27 forecast >$100B — unprecedented forward visibility
shifts.trend: FY26 AI: implied $40B+ (Q1 guide) → $56B (Q2 update); FY27: >$100B freshly disclosed

shifts.note: 6 named XPU hyperscaler customers diversifies away from Google dependence of prior cycles
shifts.trend: Q1 FY26: ~2 known customers → Q2 FY26: 6 confirmed (Google, Anthropic 6GW, OpenAI 1.3GW 2027, Meta 3GW 2028, +2 undisclosed)

shifts.note: Q2 bookings $30B >> revenue $22.2B — customers over-ordering; TSMC CoWoS capacity gating
shifts.trend: Supplier prepayments accelerating; bookings-to-revenue ratio >1.3x implies multi-quarter visibility

shifts.note: Non-GAAP GM holding near 69%; VMware blend diluting slightly but AI semi mix improving
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: ~69% (Q1) → ~69% guided (Q3); AI semi mix rising offsets VMware drag

AVGO.verdict: Strongest beat-and-raise cadence in the dashboard. The $100B FY27 AI forecast — if validated — implies AVGO captures >10% of total hyperscaler AI capex. NVLink Fusion partnership with NVIDIA validates AVGO networking positioning. Only risk: execution on 6 simultaneous XPU ramp-ups across very different customer architectures.

MRVL.headline: DC +27% YoY to $1.83B in Q1 FY27; FY27 ~$11.5B (+40%); NVLink Fusion win; 3 optics acquisitions; ~$1B supplier prepayments — custom XPU and photonics integration thesis accelerating.

shifts.note: FY27 $11.5B guide implies near-doubling of FY26 DC revenue; XPU ramp just beginning in H2 FY27
shifts.trend: DC: Q4 FY26 run-rate ~$1.6B annualized → Q1 FY27 $1.83B (+27% YoY) → FY27 ~$11.5B guide

shifts.note: ~$1B in TSMC prepayments placed — Marvell is paying for optionality on CoWoS capacity
shifts.trend: ~$1B supplier prepayments for TSMC advanced packaging/CoWoS capacity through FY27-28

shifts.note: 3 optics acquisitions and NVLink Fusion win diversify from pure XPU ASIC dependency
shifts.trend: Q4 FY26: custom XPU dominant → Q1 FY27: NVLink Fusion win + Polariton + XConn + Celestial AI acquisitions

shifts.note: Three acquisitions closed in a single quarter signals aggressive vertical integration strategy
shifts.trend: Polariton (silicon photonics IP) + XConn (CXL/UCIe) + Celestial AI (optical compute interconnect) — all closed Q1 FY27

shifts.note: GM 58.9% — high for semi-custom ASIC; Q2 guide 59-60%; approaching 60% as XPU mix rises
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: Q4 FY26 ~58% → Q1 FY27 58.9% → Q2 guided ~59-60%

MRVL.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening. NVIDIA NVLink Fusion win is the most important strategic development — it means Marvell wins networking revenue regardless of whether hyperscalers use NVIDIA GPUs or custom XPUs. Three photonics acquisitions vertically integrate MRVL into the co-packaged optics stack. The ~$1B supplier prepayment signals high confidence in FY27 ramp.

SNOW.headline: Product revenue re-accelerated to +34% YoY in Q1 FY27 (from +26% in Q4); NRR 126%; FY27 raised to $5.84B; $6B AWS deal; Cortex AI GPU consumption additive — AI workloads proving incremental, not cannibalistic.

shifts.note: Re-acceleration after deceleration in FY26 — AI consumption (Cortex) is the swing factor
shifts.trend: Product rev YoY: Q4 FY26 +26% → Q1 FY27 +34%; RPO +40% YoY; FY27 raised $5.71B → $5.84B

shifts.note: AWS strategic partnership and OpenAI expansion reduce cloud-neutral perception risk
shifts.trend: $6B AWS 5-year strategic partnership signed; $200M OpenAI expansion — top cloud and top AI lab both deepening

shifts.note: Snowflake adding GPU capacity inside its cloud for Cortex AI — new infrastructure investment
shifts.trend: GPUs provisioned within Snowflake data cloud for Cortex AI LLM inference and fine-tuning

SNOW.verdict: Thesis intact. The re-acceleration to +34% proves AI workloads (Cortex) are additive rather than routing customers away to standalone AI providers. NRR 126% and RPO +40% give strong forward visibility. The risk is competitive pressure from hyperscaler native AI data services (BigQuery ML, Redshift ML) and the durability of the Cortex AI consumption uplift.

SNPS.headline: FY26 raised to $9.625-9.705B; Design IP bottomed and recovering; Elliott board seat accelerating margin focus; Processor IP divested; 20+ agentic EDA customers — AI chip design complexity is the multi-year EDA tailwind.

shifts.note: Revenue growth moderate (~6-8% organic) but guidance raised; Design IP recovery adds upside
shifts.trend: FY25 $6.128B → FY26 guided $9.625-9.705B (includes partial Ansys); Design IP bottomed Q1 FY26, recovering Q2

shifts.note: Elliott board seat specifically targeting margin expansion; Processor IP divestiture is margin-additive
shifts.trend: Processor IP ($500M proceeds) removed lower-margin custom SoC work; EDA tools + verification IP higher margin

shifts.note: EDA is software — no supply constraint; backlog/renewal visibility is structural
shifts.trend: 3-5 year EDA contracts; AI chip tapeout volume growing — more compute for Synopsys per design

SNPS.verdict: Thesis intact. AI chip design complexity (HBM integration, chiplet disaggregation, multi-die verification) is a structural EDA spending tailwind independent of the memory/GPU cycles. Elliott-driven discipline should improve margins. Ansys integration adds multiphysics simulation and expands TAM. Design IP recovery removes the main Q1-Q2 overhang.

CSCO.headline: AI networking orders tracking $9B FY26 (from $5B); 5 hyperscaler design wins; P200 chip; Acacia >$1B/Q; Silicon One all high-end by FY29 — 'networking super cycle' thesis intact.

shifts.note: AI infra orders ~$9B FY26 vs $5B prior year — 80% YoY growth is the fastest in Cisco's history
shifts.trend: AI networking orders: $5B FY25 → $9B FY26 target (+80% YoY); 5 hyperscaler design wins secured

shifts.note: Acacia silicon photonics at >$1B/Q capacity constrained by transceiver chip supply
shifts.trend: Acacia: $750M/Q (Q4 FY25) → >$1B/Q (Q3 FY26) — silicon photonics capacity becoming a gating factor

shifts.note: Product gross margin holding ~66-67% non-GAAP; mix shift to AI networking broadly margin-neutral
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: ~66% (Q2 FY26) → ~66-67% guided (Q4 FY26); AI fabric margins in-line with enterprise networking

CSCO.verdict: Thesis intact. Cisco is validating its AI networking positioning with 5 hyperscaler wins and $9B FY26 order target. The Acacia silicon photonics business becoming a >$1B/Q operation is the highest-conviction proof point. Silicon One migration by FY29 reduces merchant silicon risk. Primary concern: enterprise networking softness (Meraki, Catalyst) masking the AI strength in headline growth.

AMAT.headline: Systems >30% FY26; advanced packaging >50%; EPIC model with 10 partners; 8-quarter rolling forecasts; HBM 3-4x wafer intensity vs standard DRAM — structural content-per-bit expansion thesis confirmed.

shifts.note: Advanced packaging outpacing core systems — HBM fab expansion is the incremental driver
shifts.trend: FY26 systems >30% YoY; advanced packaging >50% YoY; HBM wafer starts 3-4x standard DRAM intensity

shifts.note: 8-quarter rolling forecasts from leading customers — unprecedented demand visibility for WFE
shifts.trend: 8-quarter purchase commitments locked; capacity allocation decisions being made 2 years forward

shifts.note: China mid-20s% but restricted to legacy nodes — downside capped, upside from advanced node remains
shifts.trend: China stable at mid-20s% of revenue; no advanced-node exposure; restricted product set only

AMAT.verdict: Thesis intact. HBM 3-4x wafer intensity is the key structural argument: as AI memory shifts from standard DRAM to HBM, AMAT captures proportionally more WFE revenue per bit manufactured. Advanced packaging >50% growth validates the thesis. 8-quarter rolling forecasts from customers are extraordinary demand visibility for a capital equipment company.

CEG.headline: Q1 2026 adj. EPS $2.74 (+28% YoY); FY26 $11-12/share; nuclear 92.3% capacity; 5,000 MW PJM queue; FCF 2026-27 $8.4B — hyperscaler power thesis fully materializing.

shifts.note: Hyperscaler capex +75% YoY per management — Constellation is the direct beneficiary of that power demand
5,000 MW in PJM interconnection queue — demand exceeds available nuclear capacity in near term
shifts.trend: 5,000 MW data center load queued in PJM; Constellation negotiating multiple behind-the-meter deals simultaneously

shifts.note: FCF trajectory stepping up: 2028-29 range $11.5-13B implies 35-55% improvement vs 2026-27
shifts.trend: FY26 EPS $11-12/share affirmed; FCF 2026-27 $8.4B; 2028-29 $11.5-13B (PPA premium pricing kicking in)

CEG.verdict: Thesis intact. Constellation is the highest-quality proxy for nuclear power demand from AI hyperscalers: 92%+ capacity factor, largest US nuclear fleet, growing FCF, and PPA pricing power. Calpine acquisition adds gas peaking for reliability. Primary risk: regulatory delays on Crane Clean Energy filing and competition from new SMR entrants (Oklo, NuScale) in the 2028+ timeframe.

OKLO.headline: Pre-revenue; $2.5B cash; Aurora-INL PDSA submitted; Aurora-Ohio 1.2 GW Meta campus; Groves targeting July 4 criticality; NVIDIA/LANL fuel collaboration — development milestones on track but commercialization still FY28-29.

shifts.note: Milestone cadence accelerating: PDSA, Ohio site, Groves criticality all in same quarter
shifts.trend: Q1 2026: PDSA submitted + Ohio 1.2GW Meta site + Groves July 4 criticality target — three simultaneous milestones

shifts.note: Hyperscalers signing LOIs now for capacity that won't be available until 2028-29 — supply far below demand
shifts.trend: Multiple hyperscaler LOIs signed; Aurora-Ohio 1.2GW committed to Meta — demand exceeds near-term supply by years

shifts.note: $2.5B cash after $1.2B ATM raise in Q1 — aggressive capital raising to fund development
shifts.trend: $1.2B ATM equity raise in Q1 2026; $2.5B total cash; burn ~$25M/Q; 8+ years runway

OKLO.verdict: High-optionality position. NVIDIA/LANL collaboration is strategically significant — it signals the AI hyperscaler ecosystem is directly co-investing in SMR fuel technology, not just buying PPAs. Aurora-Ohio 1.2 GW for Meta is the largest disclosed hyperscaler nuclear commitment. Key risk: regulatory timeline uncertainty (NRC PDSA review 12-24+ months); commercialization is still FY28-29 at earliest.

CRM.headline: Revenue growth stable at +8% YoY; Agentforce scaling to millions of autonomous interactions; Data Cloud growing; MCP integration; FY27 $40.9-41.0B — AI consumption layer emerging on top of seat-based business.

shifts.note: Headline growth stable at +8% but Agentforce consumption model could inflect growth rate in FY28+
shifts.trend: Revenue YoY: Q3 FY26 +8% → Q1 FY27 +8%; FY27 $40.9-41.0B (+8%); Agentforce = new consumption layer

shifts.note: AI agent infrastructure (compute for Agentforce) is a new cost center — but also a new revenue stream
shifts.trend: Agentforce: millions of interactions/week; MCP integration enabling 3rd-party AI model access to Salesforce data

CRM.verdict: CRM is transitioning from pure seat-based SaaS to a hybrid seat + AI consumption model via Agentforce. Revenue growth stable at +8% but the thesis is about the consumption layer inflecting in FY28. MCP integration with Claude and other models is strategically significant for platform stickiness.
